purpose: User-invocable forked-context Opus skill (`!master_plan_track`). Multi-stream orchestration: invoking agent becomes a meta-planner that decomposes large work into multiple independent planning streams, dispatches PLANNER agents to create plans in parallel, then dispatches CHILD MASTER agents to execute each stream on its own dashboard. Decision matrix: <5 tasks -> !p; 5+ tasks single swarm -> !p_track; multiple independent swarms -> !master_plan_track; 2+ sequential !p_track runs -> !master_plan_track. Five non-negotiable rules (meta-planner never writes code/manages workers directly, read CLAUDE.md before planning, each stream gets its own dashboard, archive before clear). Defers to _commands/Synapse/master_plan_track.md for the full protocol.
gotchas: Three-tier hierarchy: meta-planner -> planner agents -> child master agents -> workers
Each stream MUST have its own dashboard — never multiplex
context: fork — runs in a forked Claude context; model: opus
Meta-planner level adds an extra indirection vs !p_track which is direct master->workers
patterns: Meta-orchestration with parallel planner dispatch + parallel child master dispatch
conventions: Decision matrix at top distinguishes from !p, !p_track, sequential !p_track
